introSection: Timber Drop is an S&S steel roller coaster located at Fraispertuis City in Jeanménil, Grand Est, France.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Timber Drop has the same layout as the two El Loco installations that came before it. Steel Hawg and Mumbo Jumbo had a slightly steeper 113° first drop, the steepest in the world at the time of its opening. It was surpassed two weeks later when Takabisha at Fuji-Q Highland opened with a 121° drop.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single car trains. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.

fullText:
Timber Drop is an S&S steel roller coaster located at Fraispertuis City in Jeanménil, Grand Est, France.

Timber Drop has the same layout as the two El Loco installations that came before it. Steel Hawg and Mumbo Jumbo had a slightly steeper 113° first drop, the steepest in the world at the time of its opening. It was surpassed two weeks later when Takabisha at Fuji-Q Highland opened with a 121° drop.

Single car trains. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 4 riders per car.
